perturbation.original_text: Non-Public Information shall include any and al l information Data Recipient receives from WECC, including, but not limited to, information received through a login to the WECC website, which: i. Is designated as “Confidential Information” or “Market Sensitive Information” or is otherwise considered non-public under the WECC Information Sharing Policy; ii. Meets the definition of Confidential Informatio n as set forth in Section 1500 of the North American Electric Reliability Corporation Rules of Procedure; or iii. Is identified by WECC as non-public or confidential at the time of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Public Information shall include any and all data the Data Recipient accesses from WECC, encompassing, but not limited to, data obtained via the WECC website, that: i. Is classified as “Confidential Information” or “Market Sensitive Information” or is otherwise deemed non-public under the WECC Information Sharing Policy; ii. Aligns with the Confidential Information definition in Section 1500 of the North American Electric Reliability Corporation Rules of Procedure; or iii. Is labeled by WECC as non-public or proprietary upon disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'information' is changed to 'data', and the term 'confidential' is changed to 'proprietary'. This introduces ambiguity regarding whether 'information' and 'data' are interchangeable, or 'confidential' and 'proprietary' are the same, especially concerning the scope of protection. It can confuse if the contract refers to certain things as 'information' but then the counterparty believes that it only covers 'data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Disclaimer and Limitations a. Neither WECC nor any owner or submitter of the Non-Public Information makes any representation or warranty as to the completeness, accuracy, relevance, or usability of the Non- Public Information. All Non-Public Informatio n made available to Data Recipient is made available AS IS AND WITHOUT WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. b. There is no obligation on the part of WECC or any owner or submitter of the Non-Public Information to supplement, update, or correct any Non-Public Information, even if the Non- Public Information is supplemented, update d, or corrected for other purposes. WECC Confidentiality Agreement 3 c. Use of the Non-Public Information is at Da ta Recipient’s own risk. Neither WECC nor the owner or submitter of the Non-Public Informatio n shall be liable for any damages arising out of the use of the Non-Public Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' and 'confidential' to 'proprietary' introduces ambiguity, especially considering the explanation that the contract refers to certain things as 'information', but then the counterparty believes that it only covers 'data'. The contradicted text states that 'Neither WECC nor any owner or submitter of the Non-Public Information makes any representation or warranty as to the completeness, accuracy, relevance, or usability of the Non- Public Information' and 'WECC or any owner or submitter of the Non-Public Information shall be liable for any damages arising out of the use of the Non-Public Information'. By changing the term to 'data', the contract creates an in-text contradiction regarding what falls under Non-Public Information and the associated liabilities. If 'data' and 'information' are not interchangeable, WECC's liabilities under the contradicted text could become vague or unenforceable if the 'Non-Public Information' is interpreted more narrowly as data, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Employees of Data Recipient who (1) have signed an acknowledgment of this Agreement or a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement, and (2) need th e Non-Public Information for performance of a job function subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below
perturbation.changed_text: Personnel of Data Recipient who (1) have signed an acknowledgment of this Agreement or a confidentiality agreement that covers the Protected Information and offers equivalent safeguards, and (2) require the Confidential Information for their assigned duties subject to the Market Sensitive Information restriction below
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Employees' is changed to 'Personnel', 'Non-Public Information' to 'Protected Information', 'non-disclosure' to 'confidentiality', 'restrictive' to 'equivalent safeguards', 'need' to 'require', 'job function' to 'assigned duties', 'Market Sensitive Information' to 'Market Sensitive Information'. This could create uncertainties about the agreement's scope, making it unclear whether 'personnel' includes contractors or if 'equivalent safeguards' meet the original 'restrictive' standard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Changes in Employment a. If at any time during the term of this Ag reement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient ceases to be employed by his or her then current employer, Da ta Recipient agrees to notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment. b. If at any time during the term of this Agr eement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient has a change in job responsibilities such that Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient becomes a Market Function Employee, Da ta Recipient agrees to notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment and to ensure that Data Recipient or such an employee of Data Recipient discontinues access ing or using any Market Sensitive Information as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Employees' to 'Personnel' in the changed text introduces ambiguity. While 'employees' has a clearer, more defined meaning, 'personnel' could potentially include contractors or other non-employee staff. This ambiguity directly contradicts the contradicted text (section 6), which specifically refers to 'an employee of Data Recipient'. Because the term 'personnel' is more broad, it creates a direct contradiction with the employee language in Section 6. This misaligned terminology creates an in-text contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Definitions and Perturbation Explanation given above.

perturbation.original_text: Market Sensitive Information a. Data Recipient agrees not to prov ide or disclose any Market Sensitive Information as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy to any person who is (1) a Market Function Employee as defined by the FERC Standards of Conduct, or (2) actively and personally engaged in day-to- day sales of electric power or other electric power marketing functions.
perturbation.changed_text: Trade Secret Data a. Data Recipient agrees not to prov ide or disclose any Trade Secret Data as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy to any individual who is (1) a Market Function Employee as defined by the FERC Standards of Conduct, or (2) actively involved in day-to- day sales of electric power or other electric power marketing functions.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Market Sensitive Information' is replaced with 'Trade Secret Data', and 'person' is replaced with 'individual'. This change creates ambiguity about the types of information covered under the agreement, as 'Market Sensitive Information' and 'Trade Secret Data' have different legal definitions and protections. This creates confusion in the overall scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Treatment of Non-Public Information a. Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary prec autions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non-Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it. b. Data Recipient agrees to not make available, disclose, provide or communicate Non-Public Information to any entity or individual, except: i. Employees of Data Recipient who (1) have signed an acknowledgment of this Agreement or a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement, and (2) need th e Non-Public Information for performance of a job function subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below; ii. Contractors of Data Recipient who (1) have sign ed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as re strictive as this Agreement, and (2) need the Non-Public Information for the work being pe rformed subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below. In the event Da ta Recipient shares Non-Public Information WECC Confidentiality Agreement 2 with a contractor pursuant to this provision, Data Recipient agrees to be responsible and jointly and severally liable for any breach of confidentiality by that contractor; iii. As required by FERC orders, rules or regulation s, provided that additi onal data recipients have signed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement; or iv. As may be otherwise agreed to by WECC in writing.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Market Sensitive Information' to 'Trade Secret Data' introduces a contradiction. The contradicted text refers to 'Market Sensitive Information' in the context of limitations on disclosing 'Non-Public Information'. The change alters the type of information subject to these limitations. The different legal definitions of the terms introduce ambiguity and conflicting obligations, leading to a strong contradiction because the scope of restricted information is no longer clear.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary here in, Data Recipient may disclose Non-Public Information to a governmental authority as requ ired by law, provided that to the extent permitted by law: i. Data Recipient notifies WECC as soon as reas onably possible of the required disclosure; ii. Data Recipient does not disclose the Non- Public Information until WECC has had a reasonable opportunity to respond to the required disclosure; and iii. Data Recipient cooperates with WECC as re asonably requested by WECC to protect WECC’s interests in the Non-Public Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary here in, Data Recipient may disclose Sensitive Data to a regulatory body as mandated by applicable law, provided that to the extent allowed by legal statutes: i. Data Recipient informs WECC without undue delay of the mandatory disclosure; ii. Data Recipient refrains from disclosing the Confidential Data until WECC has been given sufficient time to address the required disclosure; and iii. Data Recipient assists WECC as reasonably requested by WECC to safeguard WECC’s rights concerning the Private Data.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Non-Public Information' is changed to 'Sensitive Data', 'governmental authority' to 'regulatory body', 'required by law' to 'mandated by applicable law', 'permitted by law' to 'allowed by legal statutes', 'notifies' to 'informs', 'as soon as reasonably possible' to 'without undue delay', 'does not disclose' to 'refrains from disclosing', 'Confidential Data' to 'Private Data', and 'WECC’s interests' to 'WECC's rights'. This could create inconsistencies about what the agreement refers to, creating confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Term and Termination a. This Agreement shall continue in effect until terminated. This Agreement may be terminated by WECC or Data Recipient at any ti me at their sole discretion. b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writ ing that all Non-Public Information has been returned or destroyed, except as may be ot herwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Ag reement shall survive termination.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Non-Public Information' to 'Sensitive Data', 'Confidential Data', and 'Private Data' creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction. The contradicted text specifically refers to 'Non-Public Information' and requires its return or destruction upon termination. If these terms are not consistently defined or used interchangeably, it becomes unclear whether the obligation to return or destroy 'Non-Public Information' also applies to 'Sensitive Data', 'Confidential Data' or 'Private Data'. This misalignment in terminology introduces an in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writ ing that all Non-Public Information has been returned or destroyed, except as may be ot herwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Ag reement shall survive termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the cancellation of this Agreement, Data Recipient must give back to WECC all Non-Disclosed Materials they possess or wipe out all Non-Disclosed Materials they have and confirm to WECC on paper that all Non-Disclosed Materials have been handed back or deleted, unless otherwise mandated by law; in such cases, the privacy duties outlined in this contract will continue past its termination.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'termination' is changed to 'cancellation', 'return' to 'give back', 'Non-Public Information' to 'Non-Disclosed Materials', 'destroy' to 'wipe out', 'certify' to 'confirm', 'in writing' to 'on paper', 'confidentiality obligations' to 'privacy duties', 'Agreement' to 'contract', 'survive termination' to 'continue past its termination'. This makes the terms unclear to the reader.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Miscellaneous Terms a. This Agreement does not require WECC to disclo se any Non-Public Information. Neither this Agreement nor any disclosure of Non-Public In formation grant Data Recipient any intellectual property rights or licenses to such informatio n. Data Recipient agrees to comply with all applicable United States export laws and regulations. b. This Agreement represents the entire underst anding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non-Public Information and supersed es all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. Th is Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient. Data Recipient may not delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without pr ior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is void. c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement. WECC Confidentiality Agreement 4 d. If any term or provision of this Agreement is he ld by a body of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void, or unenforceable, then (1) the remaining provis ions of the Agreement shall continue in full force and effect, and (2) in lieu of each such term or provision there shall be added as part of this Agreement a clause or pr ovision as similar in terms as possible that is legal, valid and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the change from the original text to the changed text makes the terms unclear to the reader. Because the original agreement references 'Non-Public Information' and the changed text references 'Non-Disclosed Materials', the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations with the contradicted section, especially in 8b, where it discusses 'Non-Public Information'. This ambiguity is a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
